IRS990.ActivityOrMissionDesc: TO TRANSFORM AID AND PHILANTHROPY TO ACCELERATE COMMUNITY-LED CHANGE.
IRS990.MissionDesc: TO TRANSFORM AID AND PHILANTHROPY TO ACCELERATE COMMUNITY-LED CHANGE.
IRS990.Desc: GLOBALGIVING HAS HELPED RAISE FUNDS AND PROVIDED GRANTS FOR THOUSANDS OF COMMUNITY-LED ORGANIZATIONS IN MORE THAN 175 COUNTRIES TO SUPPORT POVERTY ALLEVIATION, HEALTH, EDUCATION, COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT, ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION, DISASTER RECOVERY, AND MANY OTHER URGENT CAUSES. TO MEET OUR MISSION TO TRANSFORM AID AND PHILANTHROPY TO ACCELERATE COMMUNITY-LED CHANGE, WE PROVIDE DONORS AND NONPROFITS ACCESS TO A THRIVING GIVING MARKETPLACE, ALONG WITH RESOURCES AND TOOLS TO SUPPORT COMMUNITY-LED DEVELOPMENT. GLOBALGIVING ALSO SOURCES, VETS, AND MONITORS NONPROFIT PROJECTS AROUND THE WORLD TO HELP COMPANIES SUPPORT MORE DIVERSE CHARITABLE ACTIVITIES THROUGH THEIR POWERFUL EMPLOYEE GIVING, CORPORATE PHILANTHROPY, AND CAUSE-RELATED MARKETING PROGRAMS.

SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: FOR THE YEAR ENDED DECEMBER 31, 2021, THE GLOBALGIVING FOUNDATION, INC. AND SUBSIDIARY HAVE DOCUMENTED THEIR CONSIDERATION OF FASB ASC 740-10, INCOME TAXES, THAT PROVIDES GUIDANCE FOR REPORTING UNCERTAINTY IN INCOME TAXES AND HAS DETERMINED THAT NO MATERIAL UNCERTAIN TAX POSITIONS QUALIFY FOR EITHER RECOGNITION OR DISCLOSURE IN THE CONSOLIDATED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS.

SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: GLOBALGIVING IS A GRANTOR TO THOUSANDS OF FOREIGN-BASED NON-PROFITS AROUND THE WORLD. ALTHOUGH AS A PUBLIC CHARITY WE ARE NOT REQUIRED TO, WE PERFORM 501(C)(3) EQUIVALENCY DETERMINATION PRIOR TO DISBURSEMENT (THROUGH DUE DILIGENCE REVIEW). WE MONITOR THE USE OF FUNDS THROUGH PUBLIC QUARTERLY SELF-REPORTS FROM RECIPIENT ORGANIZATIONS, OCCASIONAL THIRD-PARTY VISITS AND REPORTS, AND LIMITED CONTRACTOR AUDITING BY PROFESSIONALS. THE SCOPE OF FUNDS IS RESTRICTED TO THE PURPOSES OUTLINED BY EACH RECIPIENT ORGANIZATION ON A CORRESPONDING PROJECT PAGE FOUND ON THE FOUNDATION'S WEBSITE, WWW.GLOBALGIVING.ORG.

GrantsOtherAsstToIndivInUSGrp.GrantTypeTxt: FUNDS RAISED BY GLOBALGIVING AND DISBURSED TO MEET DONOR EXPECTATIONS

SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: A WRITTEN REPORT SIGNED BY AN APPROPRIATE OFFICER AND/OR AGENT MUST BE FURNISHED TO THE FOUNDATION TO THE ATTENTION OF COURTNEY ESKEW, DIRECTOR OF VETTING AND GRANTS MANAGEMENT, WITHIN SIX (6) MONTHS OF THE SPECIFIED EXPENDITURE DATE OR THE GRANT IS OTHERWISE TERMINATED. THIS REPORT MUST INCLUDE ALL INFORMATION RELATED TO EXPENDITURES UNDER THE GRANT (INCLUDING SALARIES, TRAVEL, AND SUPPLIES). THE RECIPIENT ORGANIZATION'S WRITTEN REPORT, INCLUDING THE FINAL WRITTEN REPORT, MUST CONTAIN TWO PARTS: A NARRATIVE ACCOUNT AND A FINANCIAL ACCOUNT.

BusTrInvolveInterestedPrsnGrp.TransactionDesc: SUBSTANTIAL CONTRIBUTOR PAID GGF FOR ADVISORY SERVICES DURING THE YEAR.
SUBSTANTIAL CONTRIBUTOR PAID GGF FOR ADVISORY SERVICES DURING THE YEAR.

SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THIS COLUMN REPORTS THE NUMBER OF CONTRIBUTIONS.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION A, LINE 2
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: MARI KURAISHI, DIRECTOR OF GLOBALGIVING FOUNDATION, IS THE SPOUSE OF DENNIS WHITTLE, SECRETARY AND DIRECTOR OF GLOBALGIVING FOUNDATION.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION B, LINE 11B
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE FORM 990 WAS PREPARED BY THE OUTSIDE ACCOUNTANTS AND REVIEWED AND APPROVED BY THE ORGANIZATION'S CHAIRMAN. BEFORE FILING THE COMPLETED 990, A COPY WAS SENT TO THE ENTIRE BOARD OF DIRECTORS.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION B, LINE 12C
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: ALL EMPLOYEES, OFFICERS, DIRECTORS AND TRUSTEES ARE REQUIRED TO EXECUTE A COPY OF THE ORGANIZATION'S CONFLICT OF INTEREST POLICY. SUCH EXECUTION CONSTITUTES SIGNING A STATEMENT OF COMPLIANCE WITH THE CONFLICT OF INTEREST POLICY ANNUALLY. IF A CONFLICT ARISES, THE INTERESTED PERSON LEAVES THE MEETING DURING THE DISCUSSION OF, AND THE VOTE ON, THE TRANSACTION ARRANGEMENT THAT RESULTS IN THE CONFLICT OF INTEREST.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION B, LINE 15
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: WE PREVIOUSLY CARRIED OUT A REVIEW OF ALL STAFF UNDER THE LEADERSHIP OF THE BOARD DIRECTOR CHARGED WITH COMPENSATION REVIEW, WITH A COMPETITIVELY SELECTED OUTSIDE CONSULTANT. THE CONSULTANT CARRIED OUT A REVIEW OF ALL SALARIES AND OTHER COMPENSATION AGAINST COMPARABILITY DATA. THE REVIEW AND VARIOUS APPROACHES TO COMPENSATION STRATEGY WERE THEN DISCUSSED WITH THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS, AND SUBSEQUENTLY DISCUSSED WITH AND APPROVED BY THE WHOLE BOARD. THE DELIBERATION AND DECISION WAS DULY RECORDED IN THE MINUTES. THE STUDY CONTINUES TO BE REVIEWED AND UPDATED REGULARLY. THE LAST COMPENSATION REVIEW TOOK PLACE IN DECEMBER 2021.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION C, LINE 19
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE FINANCIAL STATEMENTS OF THE ORGANIZATION ARE POSTED ON THE ORGANIZATION'S WEB SITE. GOVERNING DOCUMENTS AND CONFLICT OF INTEREST POLICY ARE AVAILABLE UPON REQUEST.
